title: ATTAINING PEACE OF MIND |  Bhagavad Gita 12.11- 12.15 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So today we are going to cover from the 12th chapter of the Gita. Anyone remembers the
verse number? Verse number 11th to 15th. So these are series of verses where Krishna is
trying to tell, understanding that all of us are at different levels of our spiritual growth.
Like somebody is trying to practice weight lifting. Somebody can lift 80 kgs, somebody can
lift 60 kgs, somebody can lift 40 kgs, somebody can lift 20 kgs, somebody cannot lift at all.
Am I right? So likewise, there are 84 lakh species of life.
Out of this 84 lakh species, 84 lakh yoniyas, we have been discussing how many are human
beings? 4 lakhs. 4 lakhs are human beings. And this gradation of human beings is not
basis the color of skin, not basis nationality, not basis gender, not basis your income strata.
It is basis what?
Zero.
Zero.
Zero.
How evolved a person or a soul is spiritually?
So if you were to categorize the evolution of the soul spiritually, awakened, how much
he is awakened, then you can broadly classify in 4 lakh categories.
So the bottom most spectrums are completely uncultured Barbary.
Barbary.
unregulated no conception of God almost like animals bordering towards animals we have you
know sometimes people completely barbaric cannibals man-eaters no conception of Dharma
so that is one end of the spectrum the other end of the spectrum is complete pristine pure
self-realized completely aligned to the interest of the Lord completely God conscious fully pure
self-realized so in between there are many many many many many many many levels all of us in
that sense could be of different species we think
that you know human beings is one species plants are another species no four lakh species this is
the evolution of so now all of us are at different levels of our spiritual growth and just like in a
school somebody is in the second standard somebody is in the fourth standard somebody
is in the tenth standard somebody is you know doing his graduation so likewise Krishna understands
that all of us are at different levels and hence same instruction cannot be applicable to everyone
in management also they say different strokes for different folks you cannot have same common thing
what motivates one person may not motivate another person so now in this 12th chapter we have covered
in the last session

attention. I repeat again, for one who worships me, giving up all his activities unto me, all

engaged in the service of the Lord. None of his activities, none of his words is disconnected

also some time, but then throughout the day we have other things to catch up with, other things

now Krishna is saying, just fix your mind upon me, the Supreme Personality of Godhead and engage

why the Lord is being so egoistic, think of me, devote to me, engage in me, always without

a person be devoted to him? This brings us to a very fundamental question, who we actually are.
We all understand from Gita and it is also relatively easy to come to the understanding
that we are not the body, we are, we are the soul.

mamaiv amsho. Amsho means? Pat.
Very simple. Mamaiv, mam means? Man.
Mind. Amsho, amsho means?
Pat.まり simple. No major confusion.
maaiv amsho jeevaloke. jeeva, jeeva means this living entity, is my amsho.

If it wants its nourishment, it cannot act that way.
It can act independently and not be part of the whole.

When this process is followed, what happens to this hand?
It gets its due quota of nourishment.

Why the hand cannot enjoy independently? What's wrong? It cannot because that's not how it is

some of them are drinking, some of them are drinking, some of them are drinking,
and singing and dancing and doing all kinds of, somebody is reading, somebody is bathing,

you know, they could enjoy. And then they were trying to help that fish so that, you know,

okay, you also can see and see what's happening in the world. And all along the fishes, why? Because
that's not, it's not, it's not, it's not, it's not, it's not, it's not, it's not, it's not, it's not, it's not,
it's not its natural habitat. It's not in its elements. You put the fish back in water, you
have, you don't have a velvet, you don't have any of those things, it will be happy. So we being
amsa of the Lord Krishna says mamaeva amsa jiva loke... we are designed to be happy in company of
the lord.

When we serve the Lord, we get our quota of happiness. It's a hard, that's why Krishna says, I am speaking this knowledge to you, Arjuna, because you are my friend and you are non-envious of me. Otherwise, envy means what? Why? Why the Lord has designed like that?
Why? Dvesha means envy. Why he is saying, think of me? Why he is thinking, remember me? Why he is saying, worship me? The reason why we have come in this world, because dvesha came.
Why I cannot be the enjoyer? Why I cannot be the master? Why I cannot be the Lord? Why I cannot be glorified? Why everyone has to glorify the Lord? Why people should not sing my glories?

So, itchha and dvesha. When this came, we are here in this material world and all of us are trying our best, just like fish out of water, trying our best to be the enjoyers, to be the masters, to be the controllers, to be the center of attraction.
You see, this is the natural psychology. We want to look good.
This whole Facebook, everything, we want to be appreciated. We want people to like us. Isn't it?
So, that's why Krishna is saying, the highest level is who is completely devoted, who is completely situated in its elements.
Who is the hand when it's playing the role, that is the best thing the hand can do. Am I right?
So, that's the highest thing.
Next, Krishna says, knowing that we cannot do that, my dear Arjuna, O winner of wealth, if you cannot fix your mind upon me, it's coming closer to us.
Isn't it? Krishna is now coming closer to us. If you cannot fix the mind, then what can you do? Tell me.
If you just fix your mind, this is the first instruction.
Fix your mind upon me. Because you are my Amsha.
You serve me, I serve you, both of us in reciprocal relationship, just like Arjuna and Krishna.
By the way, when we serve the Lord, it is not that the Lord is like a master and we are like the slave and chalo, seva karo mera.
When we serve the Lord, the Lord in turn serves His devotees.
That's why the Lord has said, bhakta vatsala.
Different devotees, when they are serving the Lord, they are relishing that sweet rasa, taste in that relationship.
And that's what inspires a devotee to be devoted more and more, serve the Lord more and more.
Otherwise, why will a person continue?
Why will a person chant for years together?
Years together? And increase his chanting?
Because he is getting some rasa, the Lord is giving some enjoyment, a higher taste, which is keeping him, so to say, going on this path.
So, my dear Arjuna, O winner of wealth, if you cannot fix your mind upon me without deviation, can anyone suggest what should we do?
